description: Deterministic fixture of hand-picked facts covering the dedup pathologies the pipeline must handle correctly. Each block targets a specific failure mode documented in prior runs.

facts.content: Nate Oayton runs a consulting firm in Denver specializing in tax strategy for small businesses.
Nate Payton, a retired NFL linebacker, founded a youth coaching program in Kansas City.
Nathan Wolfe, a virologist, founded Metabiota in 2008 to track emerging infectious diseases.
Nate Silver published The Signal and the Noise in 2012 analyzing political and sports prediction models.
NATO agreed to expand its eastward membership following the 2022 summit in Madrid.
Homeopathy was founded by Samuel Hahnemann in the late 18th century in Germany, based on the principle of treating like with like.
A homeopath typically conducts consultations lasting sixty to ninety minutes with each patient to assess symptoms holistically.
Homeopathic medicine undergoes serial dilution until the original substance becomes undetectable in the final preparation.
Critics argue homeopathy produces no more measurable efficacy than placebo in large controlled trials.
The UK National Health Service defunded homeopathic medicine reimbursements across public clinics in 2017.
MICU1 is the primary gatekeeper of the mitochondrial calcium uniporter in most human cell types.
MICU2 forms a heterodimer with MICU1 and acts as an inhibitory subunit at low cytosolic calcium concentrations.
MICU3 is highly expressed in neural tissue where it accelerates the activation of the mitochondrial uniporter complex.
Loss of MICU1 function leads to myopathy and neurodevelopmental disorders in affected human patients.
MICU2 knockouts in cardiomyocytes show altered calcium uptake kinetics compared to MICU1 knockouts.
Trends in Cell Biology published a 2018 review titled Hallmarks of cellular senescence by Hernandez-Segura, Nehme and Demaria.
Recent trends in renewable energy investment show a forty percent year-over-year increase in solar capacity deployments.
Trends in Neurosciences highlighted optogenetic advances in its most-cited 2020 review article on circuit mapping.
Market trends across emerging economies diverged significantly during the pandemic recovery period.
Trends in Music Generation featured deep generative models for polyphonic composition in its 2023 special issue.
Mercury is the smallest planet in the solar system, orbiting closest to the Sun with a year lasting 88 Earth days.
Mercury is a toxic heavy metal used historically in thermometers and dental amalgams before regulation limited its applications.
Mercury, in Roman mythology, was the messenger god associated with commerce, travellers and trickery.
Mercury contamination in predatory fish species poses neurotoxicity risks to pregnant women and developing fetuses.
The Mariner 10 mission conducted the first three flybys of the planet Mercury between 1974 and 1975.
The UN Security Council passed resolution 2728 calling for a humanitarian ceasefire in Gaza in 2024.
United Nations peacekeeping forces deployed to seven active conflict zones during fiscal year 2024.
NATO was founded in 1949 by twelve member states to counter Soviet expansion in post-war Europe.
The North Atlantic Treaty Organization expanded to include Finland in 2023 and Sweden in 2024 following their application.
Kriya yogi practitioners undergo multi-year initiation sequences under the guidance of a qualified master in the Himalayan tradition.
Kriya Yogis performed coordinated breath and mudra cycles during the Mahasamadhi observance at the Self-Realization Fellowship headquarters.
Plants photosynthesize by converting sunlight into chemical energy stored as glucose and other carbohydrates.
The plant cell wall, composed primarily of cellulose, provides structural rigidity and turgor resistance.
A physicist at CERN proposed a new parity-violating decay mechanism in B mesons during the 2023 LHCb collaboration meeting.
Modern physics encompasses quantum field theory, general relativity, statistical mechanics and condensed matter topics.
The lawyer representing the plaintiff cited precedent from the 1954 Brown versus Board of Education case during oral argument.
Contract law in the European Union is partially harmonized through the Rome I regulation on choice of law.
Trends in Cell Biology devoted a 2021 issue to mitochondrial signaling, with multiple articles citing MICU1 and MICU2 regulatory roles.
The UN World Health Organization, known as WHO, published updated guidelines on antimicrobial resistance containment in 2023.
